title: ZIKV NS3 helicase protein purification protocol
author: korvus.wang

steps_raw.instruction: Perform IMAC to extract target protein from the lysed cell mixture

Wash the column with 10 CV of base buffer, followed by 10 CV of wash buffer twice. Allow wash buffer to pass through completely between washes. This is to remove non-specific, weak binding of contaminant proteins from the resin for a cleaner elution. Collect washes separately for SDS-PAGE analysis.

Using the AKTA Pure system: Inject the sample onto a 2mL sample loop. Run the sample down HiLoad 16/60 Superdex 200 pg gel filtration column at 1mL/min in gel filtration buffer, collecting 1mL aliquots.
Lyse cell pellet
Lyse by sonication 00:00:04, On 00:00:12, Off for a total 'on' time of 00:07:00 at 50% amplitude to fully rupture the cells. Ensure pellet is °C during sonication to prevent overheating.
Resuspend the equilibrated resin with some binding buffer and add to the supernatant bottle. Incubate the resin with the supernatant for 00:10:00 while rotating or otherwise mixing gently at 4 °C

Load the resin/supernatant mix back onto the gravity flow column, retaining the FT separately for SDS-PAGE analysis. For SDS-PAGE samples, mix 15uL sample with 5uL 4x sample buffer, supplemented with 10mM DTT.
Elute the protein with 1.5 CV of elution buffer.
Repeat step 9.5 one more time, collecting a total of 2 separate elution fractions. This is to ensure maximum retrieval of protein from the resin.

Large scale expression of ZVNS3 was carried out using single use column reactors Large scale ZIKV NS3 helicase expression setup and operation of Single Use Bubble Column Reactors: Litre-Scale Expression of Recombinant Proteins for Structural Biology and Drug Design (SBDD) by Mary-Ann Xavier
CV - column volume, total volume of resin in a column IMAC - immobilised metal affinity chromatography FT - flow through ZVNS3 - ZIKV NS3 helicase

Run SDS-PAGE of all samples from total lysis supernatant to final elution. Stain gel with protein staining solution Coomasssie Blue and determine which fractions contain the target protein by finding the band corresponding to the target molecular weight. The target protein is expected to be present mostly in the elution samples, although small amounts may be found in the FT and washes. If that is not the case, then further troubleshooting is required.
Purify sample further by size exclusion chromatography .

Dispense 5 mL Nickle affinity resin Ni Sepharose 6 FF - Cytiva into a gravity flow column. Equilibrate resin by first rinsing with ~ 10 CV distilled water, then ~ 10 CV binding buffer to remove the storage solution.
Centrifuge the lysed cells for 38000 x g, 4 °C, 1 hour to remove insoluble cell debris, and collect supernatant in a bottle 4 °C

For construct design, see Zika Virus NS3 helicase addgene Catalog #228664 See Materials tab for buffer compositions. His-GST-ZKIV NS3 fusion protein properties Before tag cleavage: MW = 76.716 kDa E (assume all Cys reduced)= 108750 mM-1cm-1 PI = 7.62 After tag cleavage: MW = 48.967 kDa E(assume all Cys reduced) = 64400 PI = 8.75 These values are determined by Expasy ProtParam Thaw and resuspend the pellet in ~7mL of lysis buffer per g of pellet. Stir gently with magnetic stir bar at Room temperature for 00:30:00 to allow lysozyme and bezonase to start breaking down cell components.
Using 30,000 MWCO spin concentrators, concentrate the rIMAC step containing fractions of the target protein to a final volume of under 1 mL .

Remove any solid aggregates from the sample by centrifugation at 17200 x g, 4 °C, 10 minutes , then immediately draw up the supernatant with a 1mL syringe and a blunt-tip fill needle, taking care not to disturb the pellet. This is to remove as much solid particles from the injection sample as possible, so as to not clog the in-line filter or frit of the column.

The chromatogram and fraction SDS-PAGE of the run are as below: Chromatogram of the ZVNS3 SEC run. Fractions 1D3-1H1 were analyzed by SDS-PAGE to see which contained the target protein SDS-PAGE analysis of SEC fraction 1D3-1H1. Fractions 1E9-1F9 were pooled as they contain majority target protein in comparison to contaminants. The final fractions looked quite "dirty" so we pooled fractions 1E99-1F9 and passed them down a SEPAX SRT-SEC300 column for another round of SEC. The protein sample came off in one symmetrical peak and no significant improvement in purity was observed. (See attached pdf for more details on the clean-up result)

Concentrate the final sample with Vivaspin 30kDa MWCO concentrators to final concentration of around 20 mg/mL Aliquot into appropriate volumes for future usage to minimise freeze/thaw cycles. Flash-freeze in liquid nitrogen, and store at -80 °C until required.
Corrected ELN mistake Corrected guideline construct name mistake

Wash rIMAC resin with 2 CV wash buffer 1 to remove any target protein still bound to the resin. Take samples of the FT and wash, characterise content by SDS-PAGE SDS-PAGE analysis of IMAC and cleavage fractions . The band highlighted by red arrow agrees with the size of the uncleaved NS3 construct (76.716 kDa)
